questions.text: In which of the following growth phases does the number of bacteria increase most rapidly?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: It is a phase of cell growth, not cell division. Cell size as well as metabolic activity increases, whereas the number of bacterial cells remains the same
Bacterial cells multiply at the maximum rate during the log phase. There is a linear relationship between time and the logarithm of the number of cells
Bacterial death rate is equal to the bacterial rate of reproduction and multiplication in this phase. The total number of bacterial cells remains relatively constant during this phase
Bacteria start dying in this phase. The death rate is more than the reproduction rate. The number of bacterial cells decreases during this phase

questions.explanation: The exponential or the log phase is the phase during which the bacterium increases the most rapidly. In the figure below, the x-axis represents time and the y-axis represents log of the number of bacteria. Hence, the slope of the graph is directly proportional to the rate of multiplication of bacteria; the exponential phase has the greatest rate of multiplication of bacteria due to having the greatest slope.

questions.text: It is the smallest eukaryote:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Virus: Viruses are not eukaryotes, nor are they living organisms. Viruses are also much smaller than even the smallest eukaryotes. They lack the cellular machinery necessary for independent survival and reproduction
Bacteria: Bacteria are prokaryotes, not eukaryotes. Prokaryotes lack a nucleus and other membrane-bound organelles. They have a simpler cell structure than eukaryotes
Plasmodium: Plasmodium is a genus of parasitic protozoa. Protozoa are eukaryotes, meaning they have a nucleus and other complex cell structures. Plasmodium is known for causing malaria

Sponge: Sponges are multicellular animals, belonging to the kingdom Animalia. While they are eukaryotes, they are not the smallest eukaryotes. Sponges are relatively simple in structure, but are still multicellular organisms with specialised cells

questions.explanation: Plasmodium belongs to the kingdom Protozoa which are Eukaryotes and are microscopic.Viruses' classification to even living things is in debate. Bacteria are prokaryotes. Sponges are found underwater and are visible by the naked eye.

questions.text: Which of the following is found in all viruses?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: All viruses contain the following: core and coatThe core is the inner part of the virion, which contains the viral genome and enzymesThe coat is composed of two further parts: capsid (protein coat, always present), and envelope (lipoproteins, may or may not be present)
Cell membranes are only found in eukaryotic cells
Membrane-bound organelles are only found in eukaryotic cells
Endoplasmic reticulum is a membrane-bounded organelle. Membrane-bound organelles are only found in eukaryotic cells
Mitochondrial DNA is only found inside mitochondria, which are membrane-bounded organelles; hence only found in eukaryotic cells

questions.explanation: All viruses possess a protein coat, also known as a capsid, which encloses their genetic material. The protein coat provides protection to the viral genome and helps in the attachment of the virus to host cells. The composition and structure of the protein coat can vary among different viruses

questions.text: Viruses are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Always viewed with a light microscope
options.explanation: Most viruses cannot be viewed with a light microscope, since their size is often smaller than the resolution limit of a light microscope. Electron microscopes are preferred to observe viruses

Viruses do not contain a "nucleus" as per the definition in biology. They contain a viral genome in their core, which acts as hereditary information during viral replication. Enzymes, however, are present in the core

Viruses are defined as obligate, intracellular parasites. "Obligate" refers to their characteristic in which they require the metabolic machinery of a host for their reproduction. "Intracellular" refers to the fact that the host, in particular, is a cell
Viruses are acellular non-living particles. They cannot be classified into cells
Viruses lack metabolic machinery for the synthesis of their own nucleic acid and proteins. They depend on the host cell to carry out these vital functions

questions.explanation: Viruses are obligate, intracellular parasites, which means they require a host cell to reproduce and carry out their life cycle. They cannot replicate or metabolise independently outside of a host cell. Viruses, at their most basic level, are composed of genetic material (DNA or RNA) enclosed in a protein coat called capsid

questions.text: Which of the following groups of organisms is the least motile?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cellular slime molds, a.k.a. sorocarpic amoebae, are amoeboid organisms in which individual amoebae, upon starvation or some other signal, aggregate to form a multicellular fruiting body, or sorocarp, containing walled, dormant spores. They are motile due to pseudopodia
Ciliates are single-celled organisms that, at some stage in their life cycle, possess cilia, short hair-like organelles used for locomotion and food gathering. They are motile due to cillia

The dinoflagellates are a monophyletic group of single-celled eukaryotes constituting the phylum Dinoflagellata and are usually considered protists. Dinoflagellates are mostly marine plankton, but they are also common in freshwater habitats. They are motile due to flagella

Euglenoids are one of the best-known groups of flagellates, which are excavate eukaryotes of the phylum Euglenophyta, and their cell structure is typical of that group. They are commonly found in freshwater, especially when it is rich in organic materials, with a few marine and endosymbiotic members. They are motile due to flagella

The Apicomplexa are a large phylum of mainly parasitic alveolates. Most of them possess a unique form of organelle that comprises a type of non-photosynthetic plastid called an apicoplast and an apical complex structure. The organelle is an adaptation that the apicomplexan applies in the penetration of a host cell. They are the least motile since they do not possess specific structures for locomotion. They can move via undulating movements (flexing) of body wall

questions.explanation: Apicomplexans are the least motile since they lack specific structures to assist in their locomotion.

questions.text: Trypanosoma belongs to the class:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sarcodina includes amoebas, which move using pseudopodia. They are not characterized by the presence of flagella, which Trypanosoma possesses
Flagellata, also known as Mastigophora, includes protozoa that use flagella for movement. Trypanosoma is part of this class as it moves using flagella
Ciliata is characterized by the presence of cilia for locomotion. Trypanosoma does not have cilia; instead, it uses flagella
Suctoria are parasitic protozoa that attach to hosts and feed using specialized structures. They do not have flagella, which distinguishes them from Trypanosoma
Sporozoa are non-motile protozoa that reproduce by forming spores. Trypanosoma is motile and does not belong to this class

questions.explanation: Trypanosoma belongs to the class Flagellata because it uses flagella for movement. This class is distinct from others like Sarcodina, which rely on pseudopodia, and Ciliata, which have cilia. Suctoria are parasitic and attach to hosts without flagella, while Sporozoa are non-motile and reproduce via spores. Understanding these differences helps in categorizing Trypanosoma correctly under Flagellata

questions.text: All of the following organisms belong to the Kingdom Protista EXCEPT:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ulva is a type of green algae, commonly found in marine environments, and classified under the Kingdom Protista due to its photosynthetic capabilities
Euglena is a protist known for its flagellum and ability to be both photosynthetic and heterotrophic, placing it in the Kingdom Protista
Slime molds are protists recognised for their amoeboid form and role as decomposers, thus belonging to the Kingdom Protista
Common molds are fungi, not protists. They belong to the Kingdom Fungi, which separates them from the other listed organisms
Suctoria are ciliates that fall under the Kingdom Protista because of their unique parasitic feeding mechanisms using tentacles

questions.explanation: Common Molds belong to the Kingdom Fungi

questions.text: The disease caused by Protozoal infection is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Rickets is caused by vitamin D deficiency
Goiter is caused by iron deficiency

Malaria is caused by protozoa of the genus Plasmodium. Four species cause disease in humans: P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. ovale, and P. malariae. Malaria is a vector-borne infectious disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. The parasites are transmitted to humans through the bite of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. Hence, this option is correct
Beri-beri is a vitamin B1 deficiency disease that causes neurological and cardiovascular problems
Alzheimer's is a non-pathogenic, inherited disorder

questions.explanation: Malaria is a vector-borne infectious disease caused by Plasmodium parasites. The parasites are transmitted to humans through the bite of infected Anopheles mosquitoes. Malaria is a major public health problem in many parts of the world, particularly in Africa, Asia, and South America. Plasmodium parasites are protozoa; hence, Malaria is a protozoal infection

questions.text: Which of the following is true about amoeba ?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: They move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia
options.explanation: This statement is correct as amoebas use pseudopodia for locomotion and capturing food.
This statement is incorrect. Amoebas have a flexible cell membrane, not a rigid cell wall, allowing for their unique movement.
This statement is incorrect. Amoebas are unicellular organisms, consisting of a single cell that performs all necessary life functions.
This statement is incorrect. Amoebas are primarily heterotrophic, feeding on other organisms or organic matter rather than producing their own food.

questions.explanation: Amoeba is a unicellular, heterotrophic organism without a rigid cell wall. It moves and captures food by extending pseudopodia (false feet).

questions.text: Red algae do not contribute significantly towards:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Red algae are essential components of coral reef ecosystems, providing the foundational structure and calcium carbonate for reef growth. Their ability to secrete calcium carbonate helps bind coral colonies together, forming the intricate reef structures that support a diverse range of marine life

Red algae contribute to limestone formation through their calcium carbonate deposition. Over time, the accumulation of calcium carbonate from red algae and other marine organisms can form extensive limestone deposits, which serve as a valuable geological resource

This option is correct because this is not the practical importance of Red algae. Red algae play a crucial role in marine ecosystems and contribute to the formation of coral reefs, limestone deposits, and chalk deposits. However, their contribution to fertilizer production is negligible

Chalk is a type of soft, white limestone composed primarily of the remains of microscopic algae, including red algae. The calcium carbonate secreted by red algae plays a significant role in the formation of chalk deposits, which are used in various applications, including cement production and writing materials

questions.explanation: The multicellular body form of red algae is commonly composed of complex interwoven filaments that are delicate and feathery. A few red algae are flattened sheets of cells. Most multicellular red algae attach to rocks or other substances by a basal holdfast. Some red algae incorporate calcium carbonate in their cell walls from the ocean and take part in building coral reefs along with coral animals. Red algae do not contribute to making fertilizers

questions.text: Which of the following is TRUE about Amoebae?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebae lack flagella and move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia (false feet)
Amoebae are Unicellular with no definite shape

options.text: They do not cause any disease in humans
options.explanation: The intestinal parasite, Entamoeba histolytica, causes amoebic dysentery in humans

options.text: They move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia
options.explanation: This option is correct because Amoebae lack flagella and move by forming specialised cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia (false feet)

questions.explanation: Amoeba moves from one place to another using their cytoplasmic processes and by the process of pseudopodia. Amoeba are single-celled (unicellular organisms), not possessing flagella (as shown in the diagram below). They can cause various diseases in humans such as amoebiasis, which is a form of dysentery

questions.text: A student studied a drop of pond water with the low power of compound microscope and made the following exact drawing of an organism she observed, Which kingdom is the organism classified as?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Kingdom protista has membrane-bound organelles, as shown in the diagram and a true nucleus. That is why option A is correct
Option B is wrong because organisms belonging to Kingdom Monera cannot have a true nucleus and membrane bound organelles
Option C is wrong because it is not a kingdom rather a subclassification of Kingdom Animalia
Option D is wrong because the organism shown is unicellular
Option E is wrong because bryophyta is not a kingdom but a subclassification of plant kingdom

questions.explanation: Kingdom Protista has membrane-bound organelles, as shown in the diagram and a true nucleus. That is why option A is correct

questions.text: The gametophyte of Ulva is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A is the correct option because Ulva cycles between two morphologically similar multicellular adult phases, a haploid gametophyte, and a diploid sporophyte. Diploid sporophytes produce haploid zoospores that develop into gametophytes. Haploid gametophytes produce haploid gametes
Option B is wrong because the sporophyte of Ulva is diploid. Its gametophyte is haploid
Option C is wrong because it is a fact as shown in life cycle of Ulva
Option D is wrong because it is a fact as shown in life cycle of Ulva

questions.explanation: Ulva cycles between two morphologically similar multicellular adult phases, a haploid gametophyte and a diploid sporophyte. Diploid sporophytes produce haploid zoospores that develop into gametophytes. Haploid gametophytes produce haploid gametes

questions.text: Symptoms of malaria occur specifically due to the formation of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the form of the malaria parasite that is injected into the human body by a female Anopheles mosquito during a blood meal. Sporozoites travel through the bloodstream and eventually enter liver cells, where they multiply and differentiate into merozoites

Symptoms of malaria occur specifically due to the formation of merozoites. Merozoites are a form of the malaria parasite that infects red blood cells, leading to their destruction and causing the characteristic symptoms of the disease, such as fever, chills, headache, and fatigue. The release of merozoites from infected red blood cells triggers an immune response, which can also contribute to the severity of symptoms. Hence, this option is correct

This is the form of the malaria parasite that is produced by the asexual replication of merozoites within human red blood cells. Gametocytes are not involved in causing symptoms of malaria, but they are responsible for transmitting the disease from humans to mosquitoes during a blood meal

This term usually refers to the female gamete (egg cell) produced by animals, including humans. In the context of malaria, there is no such thing as an oocyte, as the parasite responsible for the disease is not an animal and does not have gametes in the traditional sense

questions.explanation: The fever and chills of malaria are associated with the rupture of erythrocytic-stage schizonts. In severe falciparum malaria, parasitised red cells may obstruct capillaries and postcapillary venules, leading to local hypoxia and the release of toxic cellular products. The invasive blood stage of malaria parasites, merozoites, are complex entities specialized for the capture and entry of red blood cells

questions.text: Select a parasitic zooflagellate:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trichonympha are complex, specialised flagellates with many flagella which live as symbionts in the guts of termites and help in the digestion of dry wood
Trypanosoma is a human parasite causing African sleeping sickness. It is transmitted by the bite of an infected tse-tse fly. Hence, this option is correct

Choanoflagellates are sessile marine or freshwater flagellates which are attached by a stalk and their single flagellum is surrounded by a delicate collar. They are of special interest because of their striking resemblance to collar cells in sponges
Euglena is a genus of single cell flagellate eukaryotes. It is found in freshwater sources like a quiet puddle or pond and can even be found in a swimming pool

questions.explanation: Protozoa that move by means of flagella are called zooflagellates. Trypanosoma is a human parasitic flagellate. It is transmitted by the bite of a tse-tse (se-se) fly and it is the cause of African sleeping sickness

questions.text: Chlorophyta is considered to be closest to plants but do not resemble plants in having:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall
Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall
Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall

Chlorophyta shares many similarities with plants, such as the presence of chlorophyll a and b, starch as stored food, and a cellulose cell wall; it differs in the structure of its sex organs. Hence, this option is correct

questions.explanation: Chlorophyta is a phylum of green algae that lives in marine habitats. Some of them are found in freshwater and on land. Some species have even become adapted to thriving in extreme environments, such as deserts, arctic regions, and hypersaline habitats. The predominant pigment is chlorophyll (particularly, a and b). The storage form in Chlorophyta is starch, and the cell wall is composed of Cellulose cell wall

questions.text: Freshwater protozoans pump out excess water by a special structure called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The oral groove is a structure found in certain protozoans, such as paramecia, which is involved in the intake of food particles through ciliary movement
This option is correct. Freshwater protozoans have a contractile vacuole, which is a specialised organelle responsible for regulating water balance within the cell
This option is incorrect. While vesicles are membrane-bound sacs involved in various cellular processes, they are not specifically associated with pumping out excess water in freshwater protozoans
This option is incorrect. Vacuoles are membrane-bound organelles found in cells that have various functions, such as storing substances, maintaining turgor pressure, and facilitating digestion

questions.explanation: The organelle used to remove excess water in protozoa is a contractile vacuole. Water can move through the cellular membrane of protozoa by osmosis, thus maintaining osmoregulatory homeostasis

questions.text: These protists are mostly unicellular (a few are colonial) organisms with spherical or elongated bodies with a single central nucleus. They possess one to many long, whip-like flagella that enable them to move. Flagellates move rapidly, pulling themselves forward by lashing flexible flagella, that are usually located at the anterior end. Examples include Trypanosome, Euglena, etc. The above lines indicate which of the following groups of protozoa:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Amoebas are not characterized by possessing whip-like flagella. They typically move using pseudopodia, which are temporary extensions of their cytoplasm
Arthropods are a completely different group of organisms, which includes insects, spiders, and crustaceans. They are not unicellular or protozoa

Foraminifera are marine protozoa characterized by the presence of a shell, often with intricate patterns. They move using pseudopodia and do not possess whip-like flagella as described in the given lines

The characteristics described, including being mostly unicellular (with some colonial forms), having spherical or elongated bodies with a single central nucleus, and possessing whip-like flagella for rapid movement, match the group of protozoa called zooflagellates

questions.explanation: Zooflagellates are mostly unicellular (a few are colonial) organisms with spherical or elongated bodies with a single central nucleus. They possess one to many long, whip-like flagella that enable them to move. Flagellates move rapidly, pulling themselves forward by lashing flexible flagella, which are usually located at the anterior end. Flagellates obtain their food either by ingesting living or dead organisms or by absorbing nutrients from dead or decomposing organic matter. They may be free-living, symbionts or parasites. Trichonymphas are complex, specialised flagellates with many flagella which live as symbionts in the guts of termites and help in the digestion of dry wood

questions.text: Which of the following results from conjugation in Paramecium?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Conjugation is a method of sexual reproduction. Hence, it cannot cause death. So, this option is incorrect
As conjugation leads to cell division in a paramecium zygote, this is the correct option
Budding is a method of asexual reproduction, mostly observed in yeast. Hence, this option is incorrect
Recombination means the exchange of genetic material. It does occur during conjugation. However, it is a step of conjugation and not a result. Hence, this option is incorrect

questions.explanation: The process of sexual reproduction in paramecium is termed conjugation. During conjugation, two sexually opposite paramecium mate and exchange their hereditary material. At last, it results in the formation of a diploid zygote. The zygote becomes the new paramecium through meiosis. Hence, cell division is the result of conjugation in paramecium

questions.text: This protist has isomorphic alternation of generation:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Phytophthora is a genus of oomycetes, which are fungus-like organisms that are closely related to algae. Phytophthora species are plant pathogens that can cause a variety of diseases, including late blight of potatoes, sudden oak death, and root rot of citrus trees. Diploid and haploid generations are heteromorphic, not isomorphic

It is a freshwater algae found floating in stagnant water of ponds, ditches, etc. It is easily cultured and has been used as an experimental subject in research on photosynthesis, as well as being investigated as an alternative source of food. Diploid and haploid generations are heteromorphic, not isomorphic

This option is correct because Ulva exhibits isomorphic alternation of generation. Both the haploid and diploid stages of Ulva are morphologically similar, appearing as flat, sheet-like structures. This contrasts with heteromorphic alternation of generation, where the haploid and diploid stages have distinct morphologies

Euglena is typical of protists that have both animal-like and plant-like characteristics. A very long flagellum propels the body, which is enveloped by a flexible pellicle made of protein. A photoreceptor allows Euglena to find light, after which photosynthesis can occur in its numerous chloroplasts. In addition to the pyrenoids, which store starch, there are starch granules in the cytoplasm. Diploid and haploid generations are heteromorphic, not isomorphic

questions.explanation: Isomorphic alternation of generation is shown in organisms whose haploid and diploid generations are morphologically identical to each other. Ulva, also called sea lettuce, exists in two forms: 26-chromosomal state (sporophyte) and 13-chromosomal state (gametophyte). Since both of these states are morphologically identical to each other, Ulva shows an isomorphic alternation of generations

questions.text: Trypanosoma is a human parasite causing:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is correct since Trypanosoma is a parasite that causes African sleeping sickness, which is transmitted by the tse-tse fly
Option B is incorrect because Trypanosoma does not cause European sleeping sickness. The term "European sleeping sickness" is not a recognised medical condition caused by Trypanosoma parasites
Option C is incorrect because Trypanosoma does not cause Indonesian sleeping sickness. The term "Indonesian sleeping sickness" is not a recognized medical condition caused by Trypanosoma parasites
Option D is incorrect because Trypanosoma does not cause American sleeping sickness. The term "American sleeping sickness" is not a recognized medical condition caused by Trypanosoma parasites

questions.explanation: Parasitic flagellates cause diseases. For example Trypanosoma is a human parasite causing African sleeping sickness. It is transmitted by the bite of infected tsetse fly.

questions.text: The feeding stage of slime mold is a:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect because Gastrozooid is feeding stage in cnidaria
Option B is incorrect because Sporozoite is a motile spore-like stage in the malarial parasite

Option C is correct since the feeding stage of a slime mold is a plasmodium, a multinucleate mass of cytoplasm that can grow to 30 cm (1 ft) in diameter. The plasmodium, which is slimy in appearance, streams over damp, decaying logs and leaf litter. It often forms a network of channels that covers a large surface area. As it creeps along, it ingests bacteria, yeasts, spores and decaying organic matter. Be careful not to confuse slime mold plasmodium with the parasitic plasmodium, which causes malaria
Option D is incorrect because Merozoite is the stage of malarial parasites that infect red blood cells

questions.explanation: Usually, plasmodial slime molds exist as a plasmodium. It is a diploid multinucleated cytoplasmic mass enveloped by a slime sheath. At times unfavorable to growth, such as during drought the Plasmodium develops many sporangia

questions.text: Sleeping sickness in humans is caused by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Trypanosoma is a genus of parasitic protozoa that causes sleeping sickness in humans. The most notable species, Trypanosoma brucei, is responsible for African trypanosomiasis, transmitted through the bite of infected tsetse flies

Anopheles is a genus of mosquitoes that serve as vectors for Plasmodium parasites, which cause malaria. These mosquitoes are not involved in the transmission of Trypanosoma parasites or sleeping sickness
Plasmodium is a genus of parasitic protozoa that causes malaria, a disease distinct from sleeping sickness. Malaria is transmitted by Anopheles mosquitoes, not by tsetse flies
The Andes is a mountain range in South America and has no relation to any disease-causing parasites or the transmission of sleeping sickness

questions.explanation: Sleeping sickness, or African trypanosomiasis, is caused by the Trypanosoma genus, specifically Trypanosoma brucei. This disease is transmitted to humans through the bite of infected tsetse flies, which are found in sub-Saharan Africa. The other options are incorrect as they refer to different organisms and diseases: Anopheles mosquitoes transmit malaria-causing Plasmodium parasites, and the Andes is unrelated to any of these diseases

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Algae increase the level of dissolved oxygen in the immediate environment
options.explanation: This statement is generally correct. Algae, through photosynthesis, produce oxygen, increasing the level of dissolved oxygen in their environment

options.text: Algin is obtained from red algae and carrageenan from brown algae
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Algin is a substance extracted from the cell walls of brown algae, while carrageenan is obtained from red algae

options.text: Agar­-agar is obtained from Gelidium and Gracilaria
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Agar-agar, a gelatinous substance, is obtained from certain red algae, including Gelidium and Gracilaria

options.text: Laminaria and Sargassum are used as food
options.explanation: This statement is generally correct. Laminaria (a type of brown algae) and Sargassum (a genus of brown algae) are used as food in some cultures

questions.explanation: (b) : Alginic acid is obtained from brown algae whereas carrageenan is obtained from red algae.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is incorrect?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Chlorella and Spirulina are used as space food
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Chlorella and Spirulina are types of algae that have been used as a food source, including in space missions

options.text: Mannitol is stored food in red algae
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Mannitol is a sugar alcohol, but it is typically associated with brown algae (Phaeophyceae) as a storage product rather than red algae (Rhodophyceae)

options.text: Algin and carrageenan are products of algae
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Algin is obtained from brown algae, while carrageenan is obtained from red algae

options.text: Agar­agar is obtained from Gelidium and Gracilaria
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Agar-agar is a gelatinous substance obtained from certain red algae, including Gelidium and Gracilaria

questions.explanation: (b) : Laminarin and mannitol are food reserves of brown algae or Phaeophyceae. Rhodophycean algae store food in the form of floridean starch. This question is tough and is included in the QBank for the thorough preparation of students

questions.text: Male gametes are flagellated in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Ectocarpus is a brown alga, and the male gametes (sperm cells) are typically flagellated. Flagella are whip-like appendages that enable the sperm to swim towards the egg for fertilization
Spirogyra is a green alga, and it does not produce flagellated male gametes. Instead, it relies on non-motile gametes for reproduction
Polysiphonia is a red alga, and it also does not produce flagellated male gametes. Red algae generally have non-motile gametes
Anabaena is a cyanobacterium, not an alga. Cyanobacteria do not produce flagellated gametes. They reproduce through various methods such as binary fission or fragmentation

questions.explanation: (a) : Ectocarpus produces biflagellate gametes. Anabaena is a cyanobacteria and does not reproduce sexually. Spirogyra produces a non­flagellated male gamete during conjugation, where the entire cell content functions as a gamete. Polysiphonia also produces non­-flagellated spermatia

questions.text: Which one of the following is incorrect about Chara?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Upper oogonium and lower round antheridium
options.explanation: This is correct. Chara, a genus of green algae, has a reproductive structure where the upper part of the plant contains the oogonium (female reproductive organ), and the lower part contains the round antheridium (male reproductive organ)

options.text: Globule and nucule present on the same plant
options.explanation: This is correct. Chara exhibits a unique reproductive structure where the globule (female reproductive structure) and nucule (male reproductive structure) are present on the same plant
This statement is incorrect. The arrangement is the opposite, with the upper part having the oogonium and the lower part having the antheridium

options.text: Globule is the female reproductive structure
options.explanation: The globule is the female reproductive structure in Chara

questions.explanation: c) : All species of Chara reproduce sexually and show highly advanced oogamy. The sex organs are the most distinctive features of the Order Charales and are the most complicated among the halophytes. Male and female gametangia are called antheridia and oogonia, respectively. Male fructification (cluster of antheridia) is called a globule, and the female is a nucule. They are borne at the nodes of short branches, globule towards the lower side and nucule (female structure) towards upper side

questions.text: Monoecious plant of Chara shows the occurrence of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Upper antheridium and lower oogonium on the same plant
options.explanation: This is the correct option. In monoecious plants of Chara, the upper part of the plant bears antheridia (male reproductive structures), and the lower part bears oogonia (female reproductive structures). Therefore, both the male and female reproductive structures are present in the same plant

options.text: Upper oogonium and lower antheridium on the same plant
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The characteristic arrangement in monoecious Chara plants is the presence of upper antheridia and lower oogonia on the same plant

options.text: Antheridiophore and Archegoniophore on the same plant
options.explanation: This option does not apply to Chara. Antheridiophores and archegoniophores are structures found in bryophytes (such as mosses and liverworts) during sexual reproduction, not in Chara

options.text: Stamen and carpel on the same plant
options.explanation: This option does not apply to Chara. Stamen and carpel are structures found in flowering plants (angiosperms), and Chara is a type of green algae

questions.explanation: (B) : All species of Chara reproduce sexually and show highly advanced oogamy. The sex organs are the most distinctive features of the Order Charales and are the most complicated among the thallophytes. Male and female gametangia are called antheridia and oogonia, respectively. Male fructification (cluster of antheridia) is called a globule, and the female is a nucule. They are borne at the nodes of short branches, globule towards the lower side and nucule (female structure) towards the upper side

questions.text: Algae have cells made up of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: While cellulose is indeed a component of algae cell walls, Galactans, and mannans are not the primary constituents. Galactans and mannans are polysaccharides found in certain groups of algae, such as red algae and brown algae, but they do not represent the general composition of algae cell walls

Hemicellulose and pectins are the correct components of algae cell walls, as mentioned earlier. However, proteins are not typically major components of algae cell walls. While some proteins may be present, they are not a defining characteristic of algae cell walls

This option includes pectins and cellulose, which are indeed present in algae cell walls. However, as mentioned earlier, proteins are not a primary component of algae cell walls. The cell walls of algae are primarily composed of polysaccharides rather than proteins

Algae are photosynthetic organisms that belong to the kingdom Protista. They have cell walls that provide support and protection to their cells. The cell walls of algae are primarily composed of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectins

questions.explanation: Algae are photosynthetic organisms that belong to the kingdom Protista. They have cell walls that provide support and protection to their cells. The cell walls of algae are primarily composed of cellulose, hemicellulose, and pectins

questions.text: Mannitol is the stored food in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Porphyra, a red alga, typically stores food in the form of starch
Fucus, a brown alga, stores the carbohydrate mannitol as a reserve food material
Gracillaria, a red alga, stores food in the form of floridean starch
Chara is a green alga and, like many green algae, stores food in the form of starch

questions.explanation: (b) : Fucus is a brown algae i.e. belongs to Class Phaeophyta. In this alga the accumulation product of photosynthesis is D­mannitol (a sugar alcohol) and the reserve food material is laminarin

questions.text: If you are asked to classify the various algae into distinct groups, which of the following characters you should choose?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Nature of stored food materials in the cell
options.explanation: While the nature of stored food materials can vary among different groups of algae (e.g., starch, oils, or other complex carbohydrates), it alone does not provide comprehensive information about their evolutionary relationships or ecological adaptations. The same type of stored food material may be present in multiple groups of algae, limiting its usefulness as a distinguishing character

The structural organization of thallus refers to the overall body plan and morphology of the algae, such as unicellular, filamentous, colonial, or multicellular forms. While thallus structure can provide insights into the ecological niche and habitat of algae, it may not be sufficient to classify them into distinct groups. Algae with similar thallus structures can belong to different evolutionary lineages and have different pigments and biochemical characteristics

options.text: Chemical composition of the cell wall
options.explanation: Algae can have diverse cell wall compositions, including cellulose, pectin, silica, or other complex polysaccharides. While cell wall composition can be an important characteristic for identifying and characterizing specific algal species, it may not provide enough information to classify algae into distinct groups. Algae with similar cell wall compositions can still have different pigments and ecological adaptations

options.text: Types of pigments present in the cell
options.explanation: Algae are a group of chlorophyllous, non­ vascular plants with thallus plant bodies. Different algae show different pigments present in the cell like chlorophyll ­ a, b, xanthophylls, carotenes, etc

questions.explanation: (d): Algae are a group of chlorophyllous, non­ vascular plants with thallose plant body. Different algae show different pigments present in the cell like chlorophyll ­ a, b, xanthophylls, carotenes, etc. These pigments provide the base for classification of various groups of algae into different classes. Members of Chlorophyceae possess chlorophyll ­ a, b pigments, Bacillariophyceae contains diatomin pigment whereas that of Phaeophyceae has fucoxanthin, Rhodophyceae has r­phycocyaninand r­phycoerythrin and cyanophyceae has phycobilin pigment

questions.text: Protista evolved from prokaryotes but which one of the following evolved from protista:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Fungi are a distinct kingdom that evolved from earlier eukaryotic organisms, including protists, but they are not the direct descendants of protists alone.
The Plantae kingdom, which includes all plants, evolved from green algae, a group within the protists, indicating a direct link to protista.
The Animalia kingdom evolved from protozoan protists, showcasing the evolutionary connection between animals and protists.
All three groups—Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia—evolved from protists, sharing a common eukaryotic ancestor, making this the correct choice.

questions.explanation: All three groups—Fungi, Plantae, and Animalia—evolved from protists, sharing a common eukaryotic ancestors.

questions.text: An alga, very rich in protein, is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The alga that is known for being very rich in protein is Chlorella. Chlorella is a green microalga that is often used as a dietary supplement due to its high protein content and nutritional value. It is considered a good source of plant-based protein and is used in various forms, including powders and supplements, to provide essential amino acids and other nutrients.

Nostoc is a genus of cyanobacteria, commonly known as blue-green algae. While it is a valuable component of aquatic ecosystems and plays a role in nitrogen fixation, it is not primarily known for its protein content. Nostoc can be consumed and is sometimes used in traditional cuisines, but it's not as rich in protein as Chlorella.

Spirogyra is a type of filamentous green alga. It is not particularly known for its protein content, and it is not commonly used as a protein source. Instead, Spirogyra is often studied for its unique spiral chloroplast structure, which is a distinguishing feature.
Ulothrix is another filamentous green alga. It is not typically regarded as rich in protein compared to Chlorella. Ulothrix is more commonly studied in the context of its morphology and life cycle.

questions.explanation: Chlorella is high in protein and is used as a dietary supplement.

questions.text: Ulothrix filaments produce
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Heterogametes are gametes that differ in size or structure. In Ulothrix, the gametes are isogametes, so this option is not applicable
Basidiospores are a type of spore produced by fungi, particularly basidiomycetes. Ulothrix is an alga, not a fungus, so it doesn't produce basidiospores

Ulothrix filaments produce isogametes. Isogametes are gametes that are of similar size and morphology, and they can fuse to form a zygote during the reproductive process in Ulothrix. This is a common mode of sexual reproduction in some algae and other simple organisms
Anisogametes are gametes that differ in size or shape. In Ulothrix, the gametes are isogametes, so this option is not applicable

questions.explanation: c. Ulothrix filaments produce isogametes. Isogametes are gametes that are of similar size and morphology, and they can fuse to form a zygote during the reproductive process in Ulothrix. This is a common mode of sexual reproduction in some algae and other simple organisms.Other options are explained below:a. Heterogametes: Heterogametes are gametes that differ in size or structure. In Ulothrix, the gametes are isogametes, so this option is not applicable.b. Basidiospores: Basidiospores are a type of spore produced by fungi, particularly basidiomycetes. Ulothrix is an alga, not a fungus, so it doesn't produce basidiospores.d. Anisogametes: Anisogametes are gametes that differ in size or shape. In Ulothrix, the gametes are isogametes, so this option is not applicable

questions.text: Brown algae is characterised by the presence of
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Brown algae are characterized by the presence of Fucoxanthin. Fucoxanthin is a brown pigment that gives these algae their characteristic color.
Haematochrome is not a characteristic pigment of brown algae.
Phycocyanin is pigments typically found in some types of red algae, not brown algae.
Phycoerythrin is pigments typically found in some types of red algae, not brown algae.

questions.explanation: Brown algae owe their brown color to the pigment fucoxanthin.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about amoeba?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: They move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia
options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Amoebas are single-celled organisms that move by extending and retracting temporary, finger-like projections of their cytoplasm called pseudopodia. These pseudopodia help them engulf food and navigate their environment. Therefore, the statement that is true about amoebas is that they move by forming specialized cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia.

options.text: They have a rigid cell wall
options.explanation: This is incorrect. Amoebas do not have a rigid cell wall. They are surrounded by a flexible cell membrane.
This is incorrect. Amoebas are included in phylum protozoa which are single celled organisms.
This is incorrect. Amoebas are heterotrophic organisms, meaning they obtain their food by consuming other organisms.

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer. Amoebas are single-celled organisms that move by extending and retracting temporary, finger-like projections of their cytoplasm called pseudopodia. These pseudopodia help them engulf food and navigate their environment.Therefore, the statement that is true about amoebas is that they move by forming specialised cytoplasmic projections called pseudopodia.

questions.text: Pseudopodia are found in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is the correct answer. Rhizopods are a group of protozoans characterised by the presence of pseudopodia, which are temporary, finger-like projections of the cytoplasm used for movement and feeding. Examples of rhizopods include amoebas and foraminifera.
Ciliates are protozoans that have cilia, which are hair-like structures used for movement. They do not have pseudopodia.
Flagellates are protozoans that have flagella, which are whip-like structures used for movement. They do not have pseudopodia.
Sporozoans are protozoans that are parasitic and do not have any locomotory structures, such as pseudopodia, cilia, or flagella.

questions.explanation: This is the correct answer. Rhizopods are a group of protozoans characterized by the presence of pseudopodia, which are temporary, finger-like projections of the cytoplasm used for movement and feeding. Examples of rhizopods include amoebas and foraminifera.

questions.text: Plant like character found in Euglena:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is the correct answer. Pyrenoid is a plant-like structure found in Euglena. It is involved in the storage and synthesis of starch, which is a characteristic feature of plant cells. The pyrenoid helps Euglena carry out photosynthesis and store excess energy in the form of starch.

Option B is not the correct answer. Cilia are hair-like structures found on the surface of some cells, primarily in animal cells. They are used for various functions, including movement, sensory perception, and fluid propulsion. Euglena does not have cilia. Instead, it has a flagellum, which is a whip-like structure used for movement.

Option C is not the correct answer. Euglena possesses a photoreceptor, which is a light-sensitive structure. This photoreceptor allows Euglena to detect changes in light intensity and helps the organism move towards light during phototaxis. However, photoreceptors are not specific to plant-like characteristics.

Option D is not the correct answer. Euglena has a whip-like flagellum that it uses for movement. The flagellum enables Euglena to propel itself through the water by beating in a whip-like motion. While the presence of a flagellum is characteristic of Euglena, it is not a plant-like characteristic.

Option E is not the correct answer. Euglena possesses a pellicle, which is a flexible outer covering or membrane. The pellicle provides structural support to the cell and helps maintain its shape. While the presence of a pellicle is a unique feature of Euglena, it is not a plant-like characteristic.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is A Pyrenoid. One of the plant-like characteristics found in Euglena is the presence of a pyrenoid. A pyrenoid is an organelle located within the chloroplast of certain algae and plants.

questions.text: Plant-like protists are called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is not the correct answer. Bryophytes are not plant-like protists. They are a group of non-vascular plants that include mosses, liverworts, and hornworts. Bryophytes have simple structures and lack true roots, stems, and leaves. They reproduce by spores and often grow in moist environments.

Option B is not the correct answer. Bacteria are not plant-like protists. They are prokaryotic microorganisms, distinct from eukaryotic protists and plants. Bacteria are unicellular and have a simple cell structure without a nucleus or membrane-bound organelles.

Option C is not the correct answer. Fungi are not plant-like protists, either. They are a separate kingdom of eukaryotic organisms that includes mushrooms, yeasts, and molds. Fungi are heterotrophs, meaning they obtain nutrients by absorbing organic matter from their surroundings.

Option D is not the correct answer. Tracheophytes are also not plant-like protists. They are vascular plants that include ferns, gymnosperms, and angiosperms. Tracheophytes have specialized vascular tissues (xylem and phloem) that allow them to transport water and nutrients throughout their bodies.

Option E is the correct answer. Algae are plant-like protists. They are eukaryotic organisms, mostly aquatic, and can be found in various sizes and forms. Algae use photosynthesis to produce their own food, using chlorophyll and other pigments to capture sunlight. They play a vital role in aquatic ecosystems and are a diverse group, including unicellular, colonial, and multicellular organisms.

questions.explanation: Algae are plant-like protists. Therefore option E is the correct answer among all other given options.

questions.text: Treponema pallidum is the causative organism of which disease?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect as Gonorrhea is caused by Neisseria gonorrhoeae.
Option B is correct because Treponema pallidum is the causative organism for syphilis.
Option C is incorrect as AIDS is caused by HIV-1 or HIV-2 viruses.
Option D is incorrect as diabetes is caused by damage to pituitary gland or damage to islets of Langerhans in the pancreas.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct because Treponema pallidum is the causative organism for Syphilis.

questions.text: Which organism uses pseudopods for the purpose of locomotion?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Correct. Amoeba moves using pseudopods, which are temporary projections of the cell membrane.
Incorrect. Euglena moves using a flagellum, not pseudopods.
Incorrect. Plasmodium, the malaria-causing parasite, does not use pseudopods for movement. It relies on vector transmission.
Incorrect. Paramecium moves using cilia, not pseudopods.

questions.explanation: Correct. Amoeba moves using pseudopods, which are temporary projections of the cell membrane.

questions.text: Dinoflagellates are known to change the colour of water to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Yellow water discolouration occurs due to some species of dinoflagellates, e.g Gonyaulax polygramma
Green water discolouration occurs due to some species of dinoflagellates, e.g Noctiluca scintillans
Brown water discolouration occurs due to some species of dinoflagellates, e.g Scrippsiella trochoidea
Different species of dinoflagellates can cause water to appear yellow, green, or brown.

questions.explanation: Different species of dinoflagellates can cause water to appear yellow, green, or brown

questions.text: Typical protozoa include the following except:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Euglena is a protozoan.
Paramecium is a ciliate protozoan, they are covered with hair-like extensions called cilia.
Proterospongia is a colonial flagellate, not a true protozoan. It exhibits primitive multicellular characteristics (division of labour) which resemble Phylum Porifera
Amoebae are protozoans that contain pseudopodia (false-feet), which help them in locomotion

questions.explanation: Proterospongia is a colonial flagellate, not a true protozoan.
